reviews.review_title: A Ghost Story without the Story...
reviews.review_description:
I fully admit that I maybe just don't get it. There doesn't seem to be much of a story here, or a real reason for the haunting. Sure, there is the hint of a real girl whose ghost is haunting the house(?), but not really enough clues as to how she died, and/or who was responsible. Also, the game is called "Sisters", implying that there are two, but I only saw one. Maybe the girl had a split personality, or her sister killed her (meaning she is not dead yet, hence the haunting) or maybe the two dolls are the titular sisters?
I don't know. What I do know is that this had less jumpscares than I was expecting, but there were still a few which I always find disappointing (if they're not earned or built up well).

The good elements aren't lacking exactly - this does have great sound design and lighting and the art design is stylish, and the whole lot together does create an eerie atmosphere. So, when I say I may not "get it", this is what I mean - this VR experience *is* startling and unsettling - so if you want a haunted house darkride experience in a more polished VR style, well this is that, but I was expecting more. This is called a "game" after all, and even referred to as the "first chapter" in the store page implying the existence of a story here. Also, when I play a game, I expect more of a challenge and some engaging context, and that is where this fails.
If there was interactivity here, I didn't notice it (I *think* your gaze affects when and where horror setpieces occur, but it wasn't consistent enough for me to tell). If this was just called an "experience" and not a "game", maybe I'd score it higher... that said, I also probably wouldn't have downloaded it if that was the case, so take from that what you will.
If you like to be terrorised by ghostly stories, you’ll love this. I was personally too scared to go very far with it, but I am a giant chicken.

reviews.review_title: Good at the time, not so much now
reviews.review_description: Some VR apps or games benefit from hindsight regardless of the genre, while others do not age so gracefully. Perhaps it is disingenuous to review an experience or game that is approaching 4 years old in an ever-changing technological landscape and compare it to today’s standards. Therefore, feel free to take my opinion with a grain of salt. But, alas, here we are. When I fired up Sisters, I was in the midst of some other terrific horror experiences. Perhaps that was bad timing, because I was less than impressed. Graphically, it looked good, but it was short in length and short on scares. The big payoff moment at the end was underwhelming, to say the least. To be fair, this was probably cutting edge at the time. I will give credit where credit is due and say that it has a nice ominous atmosphere. The setup is pretty good. But overall, I didn’t feel it was necessary, even for free. There is another app that is an apparent sequel, or maybe an expansion of this one. Either way, this experience did not leave me with any desire to pursue it. I don’t enjoy being harsh on free experiences that people worked hard on; I’m just being honest. It’s not bad, it just suffers by comparison. You could do worse, but you could also do much better.
